english: pf. gyel (gyel) (-to), imp gyel (gyel), to fall, to tumble, *gyel ma gyel* (gyel ma gyel) W. don't tumble, take care not to fall; *gyél-kan* (gyel mkhan) W. lying, (not standing), e.g. a bottle.
another form for _gyé-ba_ ('gye ba), _pyi-gros-su_ (phyi gros su) _gyés-par_ ('gyes par) _gyur_ ('gyur) back foremost they retreat Glr.

vb. (W. *gyód-ce* (gyod ce)) to repent, to grieve for, and sbst. repentance, sorrow for, not only for bad, but also for good actions, when the latter are attended with disadvantage or loss; _pyis_ (phyis) _gyón-par_ (gyon par) _gyur_ ('gyur) you will have to repent it hereafter Dzl.; with _la_ (la), to repent of a thing; _gyód-pa_ ('gyod pa) _skye_ (skye) repentance arises, I feel repentance, I repent frq.; _sems_ (sems) _gyód-par_ ('gyod par) _gyur_ ('gyur) id.; *da gyód-pa yoṅ dug* (da 'gyod pa yong dug) W. id.; _gyód-pa_ ('gyod pa) _med_ (med) I do not regret it; _gyód-pa-can_ ('gyod pa can) repenting Pth.; _gyód-pai_ ('gyod pa'i) _sems_ (sems) _méd-par_ (med par) _kyód-la_ (khyod la) _sbyíṅ-ṅo_ (sbyin ngo) I give it you readily and with all my heart Pth.; _gyód-med_ ('gyod med) without repentance, without grudging, also: in good earnest; _gyod-tsáṅs_ ('gyod tshangs) _byéd-pa_ (byed pa), _tōl-źiṅ_ (thol zhing) _gyód-pa_ ('gyod pa), _gyód-ciṅ_ ('gyod cing) _bśag-pa_ (bshags pa) Dzl. to acknowledge repentingly, to confess with compunction; _gyod-tsáṅs_ ('gyod tshangs) _byéd-par_ (byed par) _gnáṅ-ba_ (gnang ba) to accept a repentant confession = to forgive, to pardon Dzl. (p. ༤༡, ༧༡, ༩༠, ༢༢༩); _gyód-rmo-ba_ ('gyod rmo ba), c. la, to make repent, to make one suffer, feel, or pay (for a thing) Dzl.; _gnoṅ-gyód_ (gnong 'gyod) repentance proceeding from consciousness of guilt Pth.

pf. grags (grags), to sound, to utter a sound, of men, animals, thunder etc. Dzl.; to cry, to shout, _dei_ (de'i) _rná-lam-du_ (rna lam du) _grágs-par_ (grags par) _gyúr-na_ (gyur na) if it is shouted into his ear; _cès_ (ces) _grags_ (grags) so it is called, so he was called, by this name he goes, under that name he is known, celebrated; _bód-la_ (bod la) _yí-ge_ (yi ge) _med_ (med) _cès_ (ces) _grags_ (grags) Tibet, so it is said, was without letters, without a written language; Zam.

1. Cs. to number, to count, v. _bgráṅ-ba_ (bgrang ba). — 2. to satisfy with food, to satiate, *ḍáṅ-ce med* (grang ce med) W. he is insatiable; gen. only the pf. is in use: _graṅs_ (grangs) _rjes_ (rjes) after having eaten one's fill Med.; _śá-ba_ (sha ba) _bsád-pas_ (bsad pas) _mí_ (mi) _graṅ-te_ (grang te) not yet having enough of deer-killing Mil.
Cs. = _bgrád-pa_ (bgrad pa), to spread, to extend (vb. a).

1. to vie with, contend with, to strive (for victory), _wa_ (wa) _séṅ-ge-la_ (seng ge la) a fox (contending) with the lion Dzl.; _čo-prúl_ (cho 'phrul) in magic tricks Dzl.; _ríg-pa_ (rig pa) in shrewdness, cunning Glr.; _pyug-kyád_ (phyug khyad) _rnam-tós-kyi_ (rnam thos kyi) _bu_ (bu) _daṅ_ (dang) _grán-te_ (gran te) to cope even with Plutus as to riches Dzl.; _bstód-par_ (bstod par) _gran_ (gran) let us vie with one another in songs of praise Glr.; _grán-pas_ (gran pas) _čog_ (chog) let us now draw a parallel between (these two) Glr.; _grán-du_ (gran du) _júg-pa_ ('jug pa) to cause (two persons) to strive (for the victory) Dzl. — 2. in a general sense, to fight, to defend one's self, to make resistance. Comp. _gran-tsíg_ (gran tshig) words of contention, a quarrelling speech Glr. — _gran-sems_ (gran sems) 1. contention, emulation. 2. jealousy. 3. quarrelsome temper, spirit of controversy; _gran-sems_ (gran sems) _γčóg-pa_ (gcog pa) to stop, put an end to contention. — _grán-zla_ ('gran zla) (pronounced *ḍál-za* ('gran zla) in the north of Ld.), rival, competitor, equal match; _grán-zla-med-pa_ ('gran zla med pa), _gran-zla_ ('gran zla) _daṅ_ (dang) _brál-ba_ (bral ba), also _grán-gyi_ ('gran gyi) _do-méd_ (do med), _grán-ya-med_ ('gran ya med), without a rival, matchless, unequalled, applied also to things.

1. shore, bank, _ču-grám_ (chu gram) id.; _ču_ (chu) _čén-poi_ (chen po'i) _grám-du_ (gram du) _soṅ_ (song) they went to the bank of a large river Dzl. — 2. side, _sgo-grám_ (sgo gram) _gyás-na_ (g.yas na) on the right side of the door Glr.; _sgoi_ (sgo'i) _pyí-gram-na_ (phyi gram na) before the door, outside, out of doors Pth. — 3. wall, _káṅ-pai_ (khang pa'i) _óg-gram_ ('og gram) the lower wall of a house (opp. to the roof) Mil.; _gram-rźi_ (gram gzhi) C., S.g. foundation, basis, _gram-rźi_ (gram gzhi) _díṅ-ba_ ('ding ba) to lay a foundation. — In a more general sense: _grám-du_ (gram du) near, close to, just by, _rgyáṅ-nas_ (rgyang nas) _sgra_ (sgra) _čé-la_ (che la) _grám-du_ (gram du) _don_ (don) _čuṅ_ (chung) he has a great voice, is making much noise, at a distance, but looking nearer, you do not find much in him Mil.; _śiṅ-gí_ (shing gi) _grám-du_ (gram du) close to the tree.
